Operation 'AddIpToAllowList' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description lacks clarity about what is returned (response structure) and the immediate side effect of allowing access. Added information about return values (IDs and timestamps) and the security implication that access is immediately granted. This helps AI agents understand the full scope of the operation's effects.

data.current_summary: Add one or more IPs to the allow list
data.current_description:
This endpoint allows you to add one or more allowed IP addresses.

To allow one or more IP addresses, pass them to this endpoint in an array. Once an IP address is added to your allow list, it will be assigned an `id` that can be used to remove the address. You can retrieve the ID associated with an IP using the "Retrieve a list of currently allowed IPs" endpoint.
data.description_suggestion: This endpoint allows you to add one or more IP addresses to your allow list. Pass IP addresses in an array format. Each added IP will be assigned a unique `id` for future reference or removal. Returns the created IP address entries with their assigned IDs and timestamps. Once added, these IPs will immediately be allowed to access your account through the UI or API.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about what is returned (response structure) and the immediate side effect of allowing access. Added information about return values (IDs and timestamps) and the security implication that access is immediately granted. This helps AI agents understand the full scope of the operation's effects.

message: Operation 'DeleteAllowedIps' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. While the description includes a critical warning about removing one's own IP, it lacks clarity about the response structure and the immediate side effect of access revocation. Restructured to emphasize the critical security warning upfront and clarify that access is immediately revoked. This helps AI agents understand the severity and consequences of this destructive operation.

data.current_summary: Remove one or more IPs from the allow list
data.current_description:
This endpoint allows you to remove one or more IP addresses from your list of allowed addresses.

To remove one or more IP addresses, pass this endpoint an array containing the ID(s) associated with the IP(s) you intend to remove. You can retrieve the IDs associated with your allowed IP addresses using the "Retrieve a list of currently allowed IPs" endpoint.

It is possible to remove your own IP address, which will block access to your account. You will need to submit a support ticket if this happens. For this reason, it is important to double check that you are removing only the IPs you intend to remove when using this endpoint.
data.description_suggestion: This endpoint allows you to remove one or more IP addresses from your allow list by passing an array of IP IDs. Returns confirmation of removed IPs. Removed addresses will immediately lose access to your account. WARNING: Removing your own IP address will block your access to the account. Verify IP IDs before removal using the "Retrieve a list of currently allowed IPs" endpoint. If you accidentally remove your own IP, contact support for account recovery.
data.reasoning: While the description includes a critical warning about removing one's own IP, it lacks clarity about the response structure and the immediate side effect of access revocation. Restructured to emphasize the critical security warning upfront and clarify that access is immediately revoked. This helps AI agents understand the severity and consequences of this destructive operation.

data.current_description:
This endpoint allows you to retreive a specific IP address that has been allowed to access your account.

You must include the ID for the specific IP address you want to retrieve in your call. You can retrieve the IDs associated with your allowed IP addresses using the "Retrieve a  list of currently allowed IPs" endpoint.
data.description_suggestion: This endpoint retrieves details for a specific allowed IP address using its ID. Returns the IP address entry with its `created_at`, `updated_at` timestamps, and associated metadata. Requires the IP rule ID as a path parameter. Use the "Retrieve a list of currently allowed IPs" endpoint to obtain available IP IDs.
data.reasoning: Description contains a typo ('retreive') and lacks specificity about what data is returned. Added information about the response structure (timestamps and metadata) and clarified the parameter requirement. This improves AI agent understanding of what data will be available after the operation completes.

data.current_summary: Remove a specific IP from the allowed list
data.current_description:
This endpoint allows you to remove a specific IP address from your list of allowed addresses.

When removing a specific IP address from your list, you must include the ID in your call.  You can retrieve the IDs associated with your allowed IP addresses using the "Retrieve a list of currently allowed IPs" endpoint.
data.description_suggestion: This endpoint removes a specific IP address from your allow list using its ID. Returns confirmation of the removal. The removed IP address will immediately lose access to your account. Requires the IP rule ID as a path parameter. Verify the correct IP ID before deletion, as removing your own IP will block your account access. Use the "Retrieve a list of currently allowed IPs" endpoint to obtain IP IDs.
data.reasoning: Description lacks information about the response and the immediate side effect of access revocation. Added clarity about return value, the immediate security consequence of removal, and a reminder about the risk of self-removal. This helps AI agents understand the destructive nature of this operation and its immediate effects.
